summary: Advanced matching for sysdolphin/baselib sislib by implementing and cleaning several SIS text encoding, append/replace, state-stack, line-measure, and renderer opcode paths. The PR changed src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.c and the HSD_SisLib_803A7684 prototype in sislib.h; decomp-dev reported seven unmatched-item improvements, including HSD_SisLib_803A7684 0.00%->55.05%, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 0.00%->78.79%, HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 51.49%->87.35%, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C 67.41%->97.87%, and HSD_SisLib_803A84BC 66.31%->89.15%.

key_files.role: Main sislib matching work: SIS text encoding, append/replace entry handling, state push/pop stack, line measurement, renderer opcode reads, and allocator/free todo notes.
Updated HSD_SisLib_803A7684 declaration from unknown/value arguments to HSD_Text*, u8* cursor, u8 flags.

what_changed: Updated HSD_SisLib_803A5798 and HSD_SisLib_803A594C todo comments with more specific remaining diffs: regalloc/literal pooling for 803A5798 and coalesce-block regalloc/scheduling for 803A594C.

Reworked HSD_SisLib_803A67EC from dead temporaries, goto scaffolding, and #if 0 blocks into a real ASCII-to-SIS converter: scans up to 0x80 input bytes, handles punctuation/digits/letters/SJIS pairs, emits kerning prefix bytes for space/dot/digit/colon, and looks up SJIS pairs through lbl_8040C8C0 to HSD_SisLib_8040C680.

Cleaned HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 append-entry logic: formats via vsnprintf, encodes into a local encoded buffer, grows the allocation in 0x80-rounded chunks, writes the opcode 7 entry header with s16 x/y and RGB/scale bytes, bulk/tail-copies encoded bytes, appends terminators, and returns the entry counter.

Added an implementation of HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 that finds an existing SIS entry via fn_803A6FEC, re-encodes formatted text, grows or shrinks the packed buffer, shifts the tail, copies the new encoded text, writes the 0xF terminator, and returns success.

Adjusted HSD_SisLib_803A8134 line measurement: uses u8* cursor, u16 kern_enabled, s32 clear_idx, PAD_STACK(0x8), passes cursor pointers to HSD_SisLib_803A7684, reads scale/spacing/glyph data with s16/u16 halfword casts, and clears string_buffer with a while loop.

Implemented HSD_SisLib_803A7684 as a five-case push-state function for spacing, color, scale, alignment, and value/cursor states. It grows text->string_buffer by 0x10 when needed, bulk-copies existing bytes, zero-fills, and pushes bytes through text->x6C.

Collapsed the prior HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C inline-helper shape into a direct scanner over text->string_buffer bytes, restoring state for entry types 1-5 and removing matching stack entries with byte copies and zero-fill.

Updated HSD_SisLib_803A84BC opcode handling to call HSD_SisLib_803A8134 for opcode 7 line measurement, read delays/positions/spacing/scale as halfwords, and pass sis_cursor pointers to HSD_SisLib_803A7684 for relevant state-push opcodes.
Changed the HSD_SisLib_803A7684 header prototype to void HSD_SisLib_803A7684(HSD_Text*, u8*, u8).
smart_moves: Modeled the SIS data and text->string_buffer state stack as packed u8 streams instead of forcing wider s32, GXColor, or float-like accesses; this matched the observed opcode layout and reduced wrong cursor math.
Used explicit signed-vs-unsigned halfword loads at opcode offsets: s16 for positions/spacing and u16 for scales/glyph codes, matching commit-message notes about byte/float read fixes.

Kept handwritten copy loops with 8-byte bulk sections plus tail loops, including reverse tail shifting when growing HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 entries and forward shifting when shrinking, rather than replacing them with library calls.
Wrote HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 through a u8** cursor tied to alloc->data_0 so each byte append advances the stored buffer cursor, a pattern called out in the merge commit as important for matching.
Merged the pointer-parameter inline logic into HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C so pos/result/remove_size live in the main function rather than through helper out-parameters.
Documented residual non-semantic diffs instead of hiding them: remaining issues include register allocation, literal pooling, CTR-vs-manual loop form, SDA-vs-hoist cascades, and add reassociation.
decomp_lessons: For sislib, treat SIS entries as packed opcode byte streams first; only introduce structs once the byte layout is proven.

State push/pop in text->string_buffer uses low 7 flag bits for state type and the high bit for removal behavior. The implemented sizes are 5 bytes for spacing/scale/value, 4 for color, and 2 for alignment.
HSD_SisLib_803A67EC maps ASCII to Shift-JIS byte pairs and then through lookup tables; space/dot/digit/colon kerning-prefix behavior is part of the real data format, not dead code.
Manual allocator growth patterns matter for matching: SIS buffers round required growth by 0x80 chunks, while string_buffer growth in HSD_SisLib_803A7684 uses 0x10 increments.
When a target function appears to have an inlined helper, compare whether a helper abstraction changes register lifetimes; in this PR, removing the helper shape improved HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C.
Leave precise @todo notes for remaining link/regalloc-only mismatches so future work does not waste time overfitting source to non-source issues.
naming_conventions: Unresolved baselib functions remain address-based HSD_SisLib_803Axxxx names; the internal scanner remains fn_803A6FEC.
Unknown struct/member names were not overinterpreted: sislib_UnkAllocData, data_0, data_1, x6C, and x6E remain in use where semantics are still uncertain.
Byte-stream locals were made descriptive: cursor, sis_cursor, playhead, entry, encoded, buffer, lut_ptr, out_ptr, bulk_src, tail_src, bulk_count.
Reallocation and gap-shift locals use explicit size/diff names: old_buf, new_buf, old_size, required_size, grow_diff, shrink_diff, tail_len.
HSD_SisLib_803A7684 naming now reflects its real call shape: HSD_Text* text, u8* cursor, u8 flags.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For HSD_SisLib_803A5798, the PR records remaining diffs as HSD_SisLib_804D7970 pointer register allocation r3 vs r4 plus empty-string literal pooling to HSD_SisLib_804D6390.
For HSD_SisLib_803A594C, the PR records remaining diffs as coalesce-block register allocation/scheduling around new_size, old_next, free_cur, and alloc_cur->size.
Use byte-indexed string_buffer access for HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C; type 1 state restores signed s16 spacing, while type 3 restores unsigned u16 scale.
Pass cursor pointers to HSD_SisLib_803A7684 from HSD_SisLib_803A8134 and HSD_SisLib_803A84BC; the old u8 value parameter lost necessary packed-data context.
Use do-while decrement loops and explicit 8-byte unrolled copy bodies to approximate target compiler copy loops; remaining diffs are noted as CTR-loop or bulk-unroll style mismatches.
In HSD_SisLib_803A67EC, a for-loop over input plus a do-while LUT scan replaced goto/#if0 scaffolding while preserving the SJIS lookup behavior.
HSD_SisLib_803A8134 used PAD_STACK(0x8) and control-flow labels block_33/block_done to match frame/control-flow shape.
Opcode reads in HSD_SisLib_803A84BC and HSD_SisLib_803A8134 should use exact offset casts: delay case 5/6 as u16, line x/y as s16, spacing as s16, scale as u16.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviews were captured in the slice.

Automated decomp-dev feedback reported seven GALE01 unmatched-item improvements: HSD_SisLib_803A7684 0.00%->55.05%, HSD_SisLib_803A84BC 66.31%->89.15%, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 0.00%->78.79%, HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 51.49%->87.35%, HSD_SisLib_803A67EC 35.58%->60.71%, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C 67.41%->97.87%, HSD_SisLib_803A8134 82.88%->94.84%.
follow_up_queries: Can CTR-loop forms or alternative bulk-copy shapes close the remaining HSD_SisLib_803A67EC, HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, and HSD_SisLib_803A7684 loop diffs?
Can linker/SDA/literal-pooling changes resolve the noted HSD_SisLib_803A5798, HSD_SisLib_803A594C, HSD_SisLib_8040CB00, and HSD_SisLib_8040CD40 cascades without source contortions?
Can sislib_UnkAllocData, data_0/data_1, text->x6C, and text->x6E be renamed once the allocation cursor and state-stack layouts are fully proven?
Audit remaining HSD_SisLib_803A84BC commented line-measure call sites after opcode 26/default handling to see whether more HSD_SisLib_803A8134 integration is needed.
Compare HSD_SisLib_803A7684 push-state cases against all renderer opcodes to confirm the five state types and byte counts are complete.
